preamble: The rounding of [i] to [y] is a common phonetic process where a front vowel acquires labial features from neighboring consonants or vowels, a hallmark of Germanic and Romance historical phonology.

examples.note: Rounding of [i] to [y] (ü) due to the surrounding labial [f].
Rounding influenced by the labial consonant [m].

sources: A History of the German Language
